back_translation: Eliashib the high priest and his fellow priests rose up and built the Sheep Gate. They consecrated it and installed its doors, building further to the Tower of the Hundred, which they also consecrated, and on to the Tower of Hananel.
Next to him, the men of Jericho built, and next to them, Zaccur son of Imri built.
The Fish Gate was built by the sons of Hassenaah. They laid its beams and installed its doors, bolts, and bars.
Next to them, Meremoth son of Uriah son of Hakkoz repaired; next to him, Meshullam son of Berechiah son of Meshezabel repaired; and next to him, Zadok son of Baana also repaired.
Next to him, the Tekoites repaired, but their nobles refused to bend their necks down to the work in service of their lords.
The Old Gate was repaired by Joiada son of Paseah and Meshullam son of Besodeiah. They laid its beams and installed its doors, bolts, and bars.

Next to them, Melatiah the Gibeonite, Jadon the Meronothite, the men of Gibeon and of Mizpah, who were under the authority of the governor of the province west-bank of the Euphrates river, also repaired.
Next to them, Uzziel son of Harhaiah, who was a goldsmith, repaired. And next to him, Hananiah, who was a perfumer, repaired. They fortified the wall of Jerusalem as far as the Broad Wall.
Next to them, Rephaiah son of Hur, ruler of a half-district of Jerusalem, also repaired.
Next to him, Jedaiah son of Harumaph repaired the section across from his house, and next to him, Hattush son of Hashabneiah also repaired.
Malchijah son of Harim and Hasshub son of Pahath-Moab repaired another section, including the Tower of the Ovens.
Next to him, Shallum son of Hallohesh, ruler of the other half-district of Jerusalem, also repaired together with his daughters.
The Valley Gate was repaired by Hanun and the residents of Zanoah. They rebuilt it, installed its doors, bolts, and bars, and repaired a thousand cubits of the wall as far as the Rubbish Gate.
The Rubbish Gate was repaired by Malchijah son of Rechab, ruler of the district of Beth-haccherem. He rebuilt it, and installed its doors, bolts, and bars.

The Fountain Gate was repaired by Shallun son of Col-hozeh, ruler of the district of Mizpah. He rebuilt it, roofed it, and installed its doors, bolts, and bars. He also repaired the wall of the Pool of Shelah near the royal garden of the king, as far as the stairs that came down from the City of David.

Next to him, Nehemiah son of Azbuk, ruler of a half-district of Beth-zur, also repaired, as far as the place across from the tombs of David, all the way to the artificial pool and the House of the Mighty.
Next to him, the Levites also repaired under Rehum son of Bani. Next to him, Hashabiah, ruler of a half-district of Keilah, also repaired for his district.
Next to him, their brothers also repaired under Bavvai son of Henadad, ruler of the other half-district of Keilah.
Next to him, Ezer son of Jeshua, ruler of Mizpah, repaired another section, opposite the ascent to the armory, near the angle of the wall.
Next to him, Baruch son of Zabbai repaired another section with diligence, from the angle of the wall to the doorway of the house of Eliashib the high priest.
Next to him, Meremoth son of Uriah son of Hakkoz repaired another section, from the doorway of the house of Eliashib to the end of the house of Eliashib.
Next to him, the priests from the surrounding plain area also repaired.
Next to him, Benjamin and Hasshub repaired across from their house. Next to them, Azariah son of Maaseiah son of Ananiah repaired beside his house.
Next to him, Binnui son of Henadad repaired another section, from the house of Azariah to the angle and to the corner of the wall.
Palal son of Uzai repaired opposite the angle of the wall and the tower that juts out from the upper royal palace of the king, near the courtyard of the guards. Next to him, Pedaiah son of Parosh.
The temple servants, who lived on the hill of Ophel, repaired as far as opposite the Water Gate toward the east, and the tower that juts out.
Next to them, the Tekoites repaired another section, from opposite the great tower that juts out, as far as the wall of Ophel.
Above the Horse Gate, the priests repaired, each one repairing in front of his own house.
Next to them, Zadok son of Immer also repaired across from his house. Next to him, Shemaiah son of Shecaniah, the keeper of the East Gate, also repaired.
Next to him, Hananiah son of Shelemiah, and Hanun the sixth son of Zalaph, repaired another section. Next to them, Meshullam son of Berechiah also repaired across from his quarters.

Next to him, Malchijah, who was a goldsmith, also repaired as far as the house of the temple servants and the merchants, across from the Muster Gate, as far as the upper chamber above the corner of the wall.
And the section between the upper chamber above the corner of the wall and the Sheep Gate, the goldsmiths and merchants repaired.
When Sanballat heard that we were building the wall, he became angry and greatly displeased, and he mocked the Jews.

He said before his brothers and the army of Samaria, "What are these feeble Jews doing? Will they let them do it? Will they offer sacrifices? Will they finish it in a single day? Will they bring up stones from heaps of burned rubble?"
Tobiah the Ammonite, who stood beside him, said, "Even what they are building — if a fox climbs up, it would break down their wall of stones."
"O our God, please listen, for we have been despised. Make their disgrace return upon their own heads. Give them as plunder in a land of captivity."
"Do not cover over their iniquity, and do not blot out their sin from before Your presence, for they have provoked wrath in front of these builders."
So we kept building the wall, and the whole wall was joined together to half its height, because the people had a heart to do the work.
